name: Armata Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Armata Pharmaceuticals is a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing high-purity, pathogen-specific bacteriophage therapeutics for the treatment of antibiotic-resistant and difficult-to-treat bacterial infections. Its lead product candidates are AP-PA02 for chronic pulmonary Pseudomonas aeruginosa infections and AP-SA02 for acute Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia. The company aims to provide alternatives to traditional antibiotics for patients with drug-resistant infections.
sellsTo: Patients with cystic fibrosis and chronic pulmonary P. aeruginosa infection
Patients with non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis and chronic pulmonary P. aeruginosa infection
Adults with complicated S. aureus bacteremia

exposures.sentence: We are a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the development of high-purity and potency, pathogen-specific bacteriophage therapeutics for the treatment of antibiotic-resistant and difficult-to-treat bacterial infections using our proprietary bacteriophage-based technology.

catalysts.event: Phase 1b/2a diSArm study positive topline data announcement for AP-SA02
catalysts.sentence: On May 19, 2025, we announced positive topline data from the Phase 1b/2a diSArm study of intravenously administered AP-SA02 in complicated S. aureus bacteremia.

catalysts.event: FDA End-of-Phase 2 meeting response supporting advancement to Phase 3 for AP-SA02
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the Company announced the conclusion of an End-of-Phase 2 (EOP2) meeting written response from the FDA.

catalysts.event: FDA grants Qualified Infectious Disease Product designation for AP-SA02
catalysts.sentence: On February 20, 2026, under Section 505E of the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act, the FDA designated AP-SA02 for intravenous use as a Qualified Infectious Disease Product Designation (QIDP) for adjunct treatment of complicated bacteremia caused by methicillin-sensitive or methicillin-resistant S. aureus.

catalysts.event: Phase 3 superiority study for AP-SA02 anticipated to initiate
catalysts.sentence: The Phase 3 study is anticipated to initiate in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global antibiotics market projected to exceed $58 billion in annual sales in 2027 and grow to more than $83 billion by 2032.
tamClaims.sentence: The market opportunity for antibiotics is large, with recent industry forecasts estimating the global antibiotics market will exceed $58 billion in annual sales globally in 2027 and grow to more than $83 billion by 2032.

tamClaims.claim: Total antibiotic sales in the cystic fibrosis market will exceed $900 million in the United States in 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: GlobalData projects that total antibiotic sales in the CF market will exceed $900 million in the United States in 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis has 422,000 prevalent cases and 90,000 incident cases in the United States, with average annual healthcare costs exceeding $200,000 per P. aeruginosa-colonized patient.
tamClaims.sentence: NCFB is a chronic progressive pulmonary disease with 422,000 prevalent cases and 90,000 incident cases in the United States, expanding at a compound annual growth rate of 3.5%.

tamClaims.claim: The average annual healthcare costs for an NCFB patient colonized with P. aeruginosa exceeds $200,000, 175% higher compared to non-colonized patients.
tamClaims.sentence: The average annual healthcare costs for an NCFB patient colonized with P. aeruginosa exceeds $200,000, 175% higher compared to non-colonized patients.

tamClaims.claim: Annual U.S. incidence of S. aureus bacteremia in a hospitalized setting is estimated at approximately 110,000 to 187,000 cases.
tamClaims.sentence: The annual U.S. incidence of S. aureus bacteremia (SAB) in a hospitalized setting is estimated at approximately 110,000 to 187,000 cases, with rates expected to remain high through at least 2040.

tamClaims.claim: Total annual U.S. expenditures attributable to complicated SAB exceed $828 million.
tamClaims.sentence: The average cost per hospitalization is estimated at approximately $31,000, and total annual U.S. expenditures attributable to complicated SAB exceed $828 million.
